_peak_flops_tf_convention: The values below are the EFFECTIVE per-precision compute baseline used by AC's roofline model, NOT the datasheet peak. They are deliberately set to ~50% of NVIDIA's marketed dense-Tensor-Core peak so that, after the `efficiency_multipliers` block and the per-family efficiency table in throughput_model._DEFAULT_EFFICIENCY_TABLE compose, end-to-end TPS / TBT predictions match published Blackwell early-access traces. See `notes.peak_flops_source` for datasheet numbers. Override via ac-auto-calibrate when lab traces are available.
_datasheet_peak_flops_tf_note: Wave 20 (feedback #2): vendor datasheet dense Tensor-Core peaks, for implied-MFU reporting ONLY. The roofline model must keep using peak_flops_tf. On NVIDIA parts peak_flops_tf is ~half of these (see _peak_flops_tf_convention); on TPU/Trainium the two coincide.

calibration._prefill_multiplier_note: v1-fix demo-audit-2 (Jun 2026): prefill bumped 1.4 -> 2.5 to match published FA-3 throughput on Blackwell, parallel to the H100 fix. Previous calibration reported ~120ms for 7B @ 8k batch=1; FA-3 reaches FP8 efficiency 70-80% on B200, putting the real number at ~50-70ms.

notes.peak_flops_source: NVIDIA B200 datasheet (dense, no 2:4 sparsity). BF16 Tensor Core = 2250 TFLOPS; FP8 Tensor Core = 4500 TFLOPS; FP4 Tensor Core = 9000 TFLOPS. The `peak_flops_tf` field above is intentionally ~half of these (see `_peak_flops_tf_convention`). https://nvidia.com/en-us/data-center/dgx-b200/
notes.fused_efficiency_source: FlashAttention-3 benchmarks, FP4 attention newer with lower expected efficiency
notes.compute_units_note: ~160 SMs estimated for B200, verify per SKU
notes.nvlink_domain_size_note: Default 8 for DGX-B200 SKU (8 GPUs/node). For NVL72 deployments, override to 72 via an NVL72-specific spec JSON; the throughput model reads this field directly.
